summary: Iteration 7 deploy-config bugfix verified. Static checks on Dockerfiles + docker-compose.yml all pass. Smoke regression on backend API (login, /api/sessions, /api/admin/users, /api/admin/invitations, /api/auth/me) all returned 200. Frontend login UI works and dashboard renders. Zero collateral damage from Dockerfile/compose changes.
critical_code_review_comments: frontend/Dockerfile: correctly uses `COPY yarn.lock* ./` glob + conditional `if [ -f yarn.lock ]` install. `--network-timeout 600000` present. Looks production-ready.
baileys-service/Dockerfile: defensively handles yarn.lock OR package-lock.json OR neither (npm install --omit=dev fallback). Good.
docker-compose.yml: obsolete `version:` key removed. All 5 services (mongo, baileys, backend, frontend, nginx) intact. YAML parses cleanly via PyYAML.

Note: docker CLI is not available in this sandbox, so a real `docker compose config -q` validation was not executed — but YAML+content checks confirm structural integrity. Real `docker compose up -d --build` should only be run on the user's VPS as intended.

Minor (informational, not a regression): backend login endpoint enforces 5/min rate-limit — running pytest then immediately attempting UI login will hit 429 with 'Something went wrong'. Working as designed; flagged so future testers know to wait ~60s between login bursts.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 7 was a deploy-config-only fix (Dockerfiles + docker-compose.yml). Backend/frontend code unchanged. The new smoke test file /app/backend/tests/test_iteration7_smoke.py also validates the static Dockerfile/compose properties and can be reused. If you need to re-test the docker build itself you'd need a sandbox with docker CLI — not available here. The user must run `docker compose up -d --build` on their VPS to confirm end-to-end, which is out of scope for this preview env.
rca of the issue: User's original VPS build failed because (1) yarn.lock was untracked in git so `COPY yarn.lock ./` couldn't find it, and (2) no .env file existed yet so compose warned about unset vars. Fix: Dockerfile now uses `COPY yarn.lock* ./` glob (won't fail if missing) plus a runtime `if [ -f yarn.lock ]` branch that falls back to `yarn install` (regenerating the lockfile inside the container). Same pattern applied to baileys-service. docker-compose.yml obsolete `version: "3.9"` key removed to silence Compose v2 warning. All three changes verified statically.
